text:
Once upon a quiet afternoon, an old owl named Oliver sat perched on the oldest branch of the oldest elm tree. He felt ancient. Ancient owls aren't very interesting.

That is until he heard it--the soft rustling he hadn't heard in years. Something fluttered past his great owl eyes. Could it be? Surely a robin!

He flew down slowly and cautiously. There it was—an uncommon swallow. This tiny bird had flown thousands of miles across oceans just to nest here. Oliver felt a warmth inside him.

"A visitor!" he hooted happily.

But before he could chime in welcome, another sound interrupted him—a tiny squeak. There was something else! Oliver blinked. Something fuzzy with little wings? That couldn't just be... Possibly a baby?!

"Oh wow!" he hooted softly. Maybe tonight feels right after all!

Later that evening, Oliver took care of his unexpected guest. He watched over it as it grew stronger. Who knows? Maybe this
